rulings.Case Title: RANDALL WHITNEY VS. METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Randall Whitney'S Demurrer To Mtc'S Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 1, Petitioner Randall Whitney'S Demurrer To Mtc'S Amended Answer. Petitioner Randall Whitney's demurrer to the amended answer of respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission is off calendar. Mr Whitney failed to provide sufficient notice in accordance with CCP 1005. In addition, the court has found that demurrers to the answer do little to further the objects of litigation and disfavors the filing of them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION VS. APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract by AUCRA and overruled as to the second and third causes of action for breach of contract and quasi-contract by CIC. The RPA is illegal and unenforceable as a matter of law because it is a collateral agreement that modifies the obligations of the insured and was not filed with the California Department of Insurance as required by Insurance Code11658 and 10 CCR 2268(b)). While Nielsen Contracting v. Applied Underwriters, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1096 did not reach the issue of the RPA's validity beyond the delegation clause and the arbitration provision, the logic of that decision extends to the entire RPA. While the Insurance Commissioner's determinations in Matter of Shasta Linen Supply, Inc. are not binding, they provide persuasive reasoning to hold that the RPA is unenforceable as a matter of law. CIC has sufficiently alleged both a breach of contract claim under the policies and an alternative quasi-contract claim in the event that the contracts that form the bases of the breach of contract claim are determined to be unenforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFITA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: FAHAD GHANI VS. UBER TAXI CAB LLC DBA GOLD STAR TAXI CAB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Summons And First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FAHAD GHANI's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Summons And First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Fahad Ghani's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings requires that this motion be granted. Gateway Insurance Company's contention that Mr. Ghani will not be able to establish liability against Mr. Alshuraidah is not a basis to deny leave to amend. In the event that Mr. Alshuraidah appears in this case, he can file any appropriate motion challenging liability, including a motion per CCP 128.7. The first amended complaint must be filed no later than December 21, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Ghani is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SAN ANTONIO SHOE INC A CORPORATION VS. T DAY, CORP A CORPORATION DBA NEW STEP #2, AKA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanction For Disobeying Court Order To Provide Discovery, For Attorneys Fees And For Alternative Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 1 PLAINTIFF SAN ANTONIO SHOE INC's For Terminating Sanction For Disobeying Court Order To Provide Discovery, For Attorneys Fees And For Alternative Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is denied. The notice of motion recites that an order is sought against James Barchard, but the proof of service does not indicate he was served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT SEE'S CANDIES, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant See's Candies, Inc.'s demurrer to all six causes of action and the class allegations in the first amended complaint is off calendar without prejudice to being re-noticed after a decision on a petition to coordinate this case with the case pending in Los Angeles Superior Court. To foster judicial economy and avoid the possibility of inconsistent rulings, the court exercises its inherent authority to stay all activities in this case pending a decision on a petition to coordinate. See's is required to file a coordination petition within thirty days or the stay will be lifted. rulings.Case Title: LEAH RICHARDS VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Special Interrogatory Responses From Defendant Bmw Od North America, Llc And A Request For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 2 PLAINTIFF LEAH RICHARDS' Motion To Compel Further Special Interrogatory Responses From Defendant Bmw Od North America, LLC And A Request For Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Defendant has not submitted any evidence that responding to these two interrogatories is burdensome and oppressive. The interrogatories seem reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. It is up to the trial court to determine if such evidence is ultimately admissible. Defendant shall serve code compliant responses with 30 days. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $2,765 plus $60 costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. SULLIVAN VS. CANDEX SOLUTIONS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Based On Improper Forum Or, In The Alternative, Compel Arbitration And Stay Civil Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 12, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT CANDEX SOLUTIONS, INC., JEREMY LAPPIN's Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Based On Improper Forum Or, In The Alternative, Compel Arbitration And Stay Civil Action; Defendants Candex Solutions, Inc. and Jeremy Lappin's alternative motion to compel arbitration and stay this case pending the completion of arbitration proceedings is granted. The Referral Agreement contains an enforceable arbitration clause and plaintiff David Sullivan is obligated to arbitrate his claims in New York City pursuant to JAMS' rules. Mr. Sullivan's argument that the arbitration clause did not survive termination of the agreement lacks merit. Section 4.1 provides: "This Agreement shall persist until terminated by written notice, delivered by either party, provided that Sections 1.2-1.6 and Articles 4-5 shall survive." The record on this motion is silent regarding whether the agreement was ever terminated "by written notice" in accordance with section 4.1. More fundamentally, Mr. Sullivan's position that the parties' dispute resolution process does not survive termination of the agreement is absurd and commercially unreasonable. The New York dispute resolution process was supposed to apply to "[a]ll disputes arising from or relating to this Agreement." (Referral Agreement, Section 2.1.) Most commercial disputes arise after a party breaches a contract and/or terminates the agreement. "The language of a contract is to govern its interpretation, if the language is clear and explicit, and does not involve an absurdity." (Civil Code 1638.) New York law is in accord with this canon of construction. "[A] contract should not be interpreted to produce a result that is absurd, commercially unreasonable or contrary to the reasonable expectations of the parties." (Greenwich Capital Fin. Prods., Inc. v. Negrin, 74 A.D.3d 413, 415 (internal quotation marks and citation omitted).) Upholding the parties' arbitration agreement comports with the liberal federal policy favoring arbitration agreements. (Moses H. Cone Memorial Hospital v. Mercury Constr. Corp. (1983) 460 U.S. 1, 24-25.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Candex is required to prepare a proposed which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
